creator: vk7rb@internode.on.net
text:
I have been having the same problem for quite some time with FreeBSD 
6.2 as well, however, I did manage to build it on another system 
running with a lot less on it, don't ask me how.

After having been frustrated for some time I got desperate and seeing 
as firebird2-client conflicts with firebird-client (1), I decided to 
delete both client and server version 1 from the system.

Updating the database I am not even going to go into as I am still 
trying to get my head around how to do that so you will have to work 
that one out for yourselves.

However, after deleting both server and client of version 1, building 
the version 2 client which has to be built first as a normal user as 
instructed actually worked and I was then able to install it as well 
as version 2 of the server and php5-interbase which was also refusing 
to install on account of it wanting version 2 of firebird-client.

Don't ask me why, I don't know, but having got it to work, perhaps 
someone else who is more into the intricacies of the system can work out why.

Incidentally, the systems I am running are well maintained and are 
kept well up to date with respect to 6.2 stable.

Yes I am a rank amateur, however, I hope the above may help while a 
fix is being worked out.

Regards,
Robert McKenzie.
